questionDefinitions.promptLabel: What kind of symptom is the main concern?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: This one answer selects the canonical symptom schema without creating a second hidden request type.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: Where is the chest or breathing discomfort most noticeable?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: This bounded answer supports red-flag screening. If the branch disappears later, the old answer stays in audit only and requires review confirmation.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: How well do you know when it started?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: Phase 1 allows exact, approximate, or unknown onset without inferring hidden meaning from free text.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: What exact date did it start?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: The exact date is useful when known, but the branch can be safely superseded if the patient later changes to approximate or unknown.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: Roughly when did it start?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: Approximate onset is a first-class schema state, not a degraded fallback.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: Is it getting worse right now?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: This answer feeds immediate safety posture. It must never disappear silently.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: Which of these best describes the impact?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: Severity clues are bounded so the review summary and normalized payload stay aligned.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: Tell us anything else that will help the practice understand the problem.
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: Narrative provides context, but it does not replace the typed symptom fields.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: What is the main medication issue?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: The medication schema starts with one bounded query type instead of inferring medication intent from free text.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: Do you know the medicine name?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: Unknown medicine names are explicit schema states. We never ask the patient to invent a medicine name.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: What is the medicine name?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: The named medicine stays active only while the patient says they know it. If hidden later, the old answer moves to audit only.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: Why is the name unknown or unsure?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: The unknown-name branch captures a bounded reason instead of letting the schema collapse to null.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: What has happened with the medicine?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: The short narrative gives context, but the main medication meaning remains in the bounded fields.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: How urgent does it feel right now?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: Medication urgency stays explicit because the later red-flag gate cannot infer urgency safely from narrative alone.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: What kind of admin help do you need?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: Phase 1 admin requests stay bounded to one support type so later handling can route cleanly.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: Is there a deadline we should know about?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: Deadlines are bounded and optional. Unknown is explicit, not an empty field that changes meaning later.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: What is the deadline date?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: The deadline field appears only when the patient says one exists. Hidden answers are superseded, not silently retained as active.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: Do you already have a reference number or letter code?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: Reference numbers are optional routing hints, not a second admin schema.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: What is the reference number?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: The reference stays active only while the patient says it exists for this admin request.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: Tell us the practical detail we need to handle the request.
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: Narrative adds context for the admin team without becoming the hidden source of typed meaning.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: What kind of result are you asking about?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: Result context is bounded so the later summary and route handling do not depend on prose-only interpretation.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: If you know the test name, add it here.
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: The test name may be known or unknown, but that posture is bounded in the schema instead of disappearing into free text.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: Do you know when the test or result happened?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: Result dates use explicit exact, approximate, not-sure, or unknown states so drafts stay migratable and honest.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: What date or rough date was it?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: The date field appears only when the patient says the date is known or approximate.

questionDefinitions.promptLabel: What do you need to know about the result?
questionDefinitions.whyWeAsk: The patient question stays separate from test metadata so summary and routing keep their own meaning.

summaryRenderers.purpose: Render the selected symptom category in the patient-facing review card and normalized summary.
Render the bounded safety-location answer when chest or breathing symptoms are declared.
Render an exact onset date in a concise review line.
Render an approximate onset window when the exact date is not known.
Surface a bounded yes/no symptom worsening statement in review and triage payloads.
Collapse selected severity clues into a short comma-separated review line.
Render a trimmed patient narrative excerpt without inferring structured meaning from prose alone.
Render the medication request class as a concise intake label.
Render the named medicine when supplied.
Render the bounded medicine-name unknown reason when the patient cannot identify the medicine.
Render a trimmed medication issue narrative excerpt.
Render the medication urgency band without overstating triage posture.
Render the chosen admin work type as the review heading.
Render whether a patient-reported admin deadline exists.
Render the known admin deadline date when present.
Render whether the patient has a previous admin reference to quote.
Render the patient-provided reference number when present.
Render a trimmed admin-details excerpt.
Render the bounded test or investigation context as the review heading.
Render the named investigation or the bounded unknown posture.
Render whether a result date is known, approximate, or unknown.
Render the patient-known result date or approximate date window.
Render a trimmed explanation of what the patient wants to know about the result.

supersessionPolicies.description: The question is not branch-hidden within Phase 1; it remains active while its request type stays active.
When the controlling answer hides the field, retain the old answer for audit, remove it from the active summary and payload, and require a fresh answer if the field reappears.
When the controlling answer hides the field, supersede it for audit, remove it from the active payload and summary, and force review confirmation because the hidden answer was safety relevant.
Changing the primary request type after branch answers exist is forbidden without an explicit confirm-and-supersede acknowledgment. The prior branch remains in audit history only.

unknownHandlingPolicies.notes: Medicine names do not collapse to free text nulls. Unknown or unsure posture is explicit and routes to one bounded reason field.
Result dates may be exact or approximate when known; otherwise the patient must stay inside the bounded not-sure or unknown states.
Onset answers may be exact, approximate, or unknown, but the schema never infers a date from narrative prose.

helpContent.headline: Choose the main symptom family only
helpContent.body: Pick the main concern. Supporting detail belongs in the structured answers and short narrative, not in a second request type.

helpContent.headline: We ask location only for safety screening
helpContent.body: This answer stays bounded because it is used for immediate safety relevance, not diagnosis.

helpContent.headline: Exact or approximate is enough
helpContent.body: If you do not know the exact onset date, use the approximate window or unknown option. We do not require invented dates.

helpContent.headline: Select only what is happening now
helpContent.body: Choose the severity clues that best describe the current issue so the summary and triage payload stay consistent.

helpContent.headline: Pick the main medication question
helpContent.body: Choose one main medication problem for the draft. A different medication topic requires a new request type or follow-up request.

helpContent.headline: Unknown medicine names are allowed
helpContent.body: If you do not know the medicine name, mark it as unknown or unsure instead of guessing.

helpContent.headline: Choose the admin outcome you need
helpContent.body: Select the form, letter, result clarification, or other admin support you are asking for.

helpContent.headline: Deadlines are optional but bounded
helpContent.body: Only add a deadline if one exists. If you do not know it, keep the explicit no-deadline state.

helpContent.headline: Pick the result context you recognise
helpContent.body: Choose the type of test or investigation if you know it. The name field can remain unknown without breaking the schema.

helpContent.headline: Approximate result dates are acceptable
helpContent.body: If you only know roughly when the result happened, use the approximate date field. Unknown is a valid explicit state.

helpContent.headline: Narrative supports the structured answers
helpContent.body: Narrative gives context, but the structured fields remain the only source for typed summary, validation, and normalization.
